metadata.title: The Inner Sphere Paradox: It Pokes Outside the Box in 10D

beats.narration_text: Merhaba. A learner asks: build me an interactive simulation of the inner sphere paradox: it pokes outside the box in 10d. One Claude prompt. Here is what comes back.

props.command: build me an interactive sim of the inner sphere paradox: it pokes outside the box in 10d
output: ✓ The Inner Sphere Paradox: It Pokes Outside the Box in 10D

beats.narration_text: The Inner Sphere Paradox: It Pokes Outside the Box in 10D is one of those ideas that is easier to see than to state. Pack unit spheres in the corners of an n-dimensional box. Drop one sphere in the middle — by dimension 10, it pokes through the walls.. This simulation makes that visible — and every educator can build one.
beats.new_visual_element: Concept card: The Inner Sphere Paradox: It Pokes Outside the Box in 10D

beats.narration_text: Here is the prompt. One message to Claude.
beats.code_snippet: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of The Inner Sphere Paradox: It Pokes Outside the Box in 10D. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: Dimension n. Verify: Pack unit spheres in the corners of an n-dimensional box. Drop one sphere in the middle — by dimension 10, it pokes thro. Palette: background #FFFFFF, ink #2A1A0E, accent #C8102E.

props.command: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of The Inner Sphere Paradox: It Pokes Outside the Box in 10D. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: Dimension n.

beats.narration_text: Claude returns a single-file HTML. Here is the heart of it — the integrator and the slider handler. No server. No build step. One file, everything in the browser.
beats.code_snippet: // The Inner Sphere Paradox: It Pokes Outside the Box in 10D — core physics
beats.new_visual_element: Onda code-block: core JS (integrator + slider handler)
beats.prompt: Show the core physics loop from inner-sphere-paradox.html

beats.narration_text: At the default settings: the inner sphere paradox: it pokes outside the box in 10d. Watch what the simulation shows.
Now drag Dimension n from 2 to 20. Watch the physics respond in real time.
beats.new_visual_element: Slate: narrate slider sweep of Dimension n from 2 to 20

beats.narration_text: Pack unit spheres in the corners of an n-dimensional box. Drop one sphere in the middle — by dimension 10, it pokes through the walls.. Exactly as the equations predict.

One prompt. A live demonstration of the inner sphere paradox: it pokes outside the box in 10d. For educators: every concept in your course can have one of these. Claude builds it; you judge it; your students explore it.
Open this file in your browser and drag the controls. Or ask Claude to add a second parameter, change the palette, or explain the physics step by step alongside the simulation.
Take this prompt, run it on your own topic. Your turn.
beats.code_snippet: Take this The Inner Sphere Paradox: It Pokes Outside the Box in 10D simulation and add a second slider that controls Dimension n. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.
